# virelia

> Privacy guardrail for MCP tool traffic and Claude Code: detects PII
> (India-first: Aadhaar/Verhoeff, PAN, IFSC; plus emails, phones, cards/Luhn,
> credentials), applies declarative YAML policies (allow/partial_mask/mask/
> tokenize/redact/block), keeps a hash-only audit trail. Apache-2.0,
> self-hostable, no external services.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): pitch, quickstart, Claude Code plugin install
- [Claude Code plugin](docs/claude-plugin.md): the three guards, proxy setup, config
- [Policy schema](docs/policy-schema.md): YAML reference, actions, json_path grammar, entity types
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): tiers, scan pipeline, adapter contract
- [Vault](docs/vault.md): reversible tokenization — stable [ENTITY_N] tokens, virelia rehydrate
- [Threat model](docs/threat-model.md): what it protects against and what it does not

## Code

- [Engine](src/virelia/engine.py): scan_request/scan_result over plain JSON payloads
- [Detectors](src/virelia/detectors/): regex + checksum entity detectors, column-name heuristics
- [Hooks](src/virelia/hooks/): Claude Code hook handlers (prompt/tool-input/tool-output guards)
- [Proxy](src/virelia/adapters/proxy.py): masking MCP gateway (fastmcp)
- [Vault](src/virelia/vault/store.py): SQLite token store, Fernet-encrypted values
- [Presets](src/virelia/policies/): personal, ngo-default, strict, dev
